title: possible scheduling semantics violation or `<=` / `=` confusion

features.name: task_inout_blocking_assignment_in_always_ff
features.description: Code should include a task with an inout argument modified by a blocking assignment, called within an always_ff block, where the modified variable is also used in a subsequent non-blocking assignment.

features.name: task_copyout_blocks_before_subsequent_nba
features.description: Code should include a pattern where a task's inout argument copy-out (which behaves as a blocking assignment per IEEE 1800-2023 Section 4.9.7) must complete before a subsequent non-blocking assignment reads the variable.
features.snippet:
always_ff @(posedge clk) begin
    var_a <= data_in;
    modify_task(var_a);  // copy-out is blocking, must be visible
    var_b <= var_a;       // NBA scheduled after blocking copy-out
end

features.name: mixed_blocking_nba_same_variable_always_ff
features.description: Code should include an always_ff block where the same variable is assigned via both non-blocking assignment and blocking assignment (via task inout), with another variable reading the blocking-assigned value via NBA.
features.snippet:
always_ff @(posedge clk) begin
    reg_a <= data_in;     // NBA scheduled
    task_modify(reg_a);   // blocking modify via inout
    reg_b <= reg_a;       // should capture blocking-modified value
end
